Motion To Compel Further Answers At Deposition
Off Calendar Nov-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SHERRY DENHAM-JACKSON Motion To Compel Further Answers At Deposition

Denham-Jackson's Motion to Compel Further Answers at Deposition is DENIED. The objections by counsel for the witness Kim asserting the attorney client privilege and necessarily the attorney work product doctrine are sustained as are the tacit objections asserted by the witness, Kim on the same grounds. The fact that Kim sat in on the hearing panel for the executive director position to which Denham-Jackson applied does not waive the attorney client privilege as Denham-Jackson broadly supposes. Denham-Jackson's efforts to characterize Kim as an "employee" as illustrated in the case of Alpha Beta Co. v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 818, 824-825 are unpersuasive. Kim's position was Senior Deputy General Counsel for SFUSD and the Court, having read all excerpts (including those not highlighted) provided of the deposition finds that the questions from Denham-Jackson's attorney sought to invade the attorney-client privilege. Kim's assumed knowledge of the reasons Denham-Jackson was not chosen for the executive director position Denham-Jackson applied for are necessarily part of Kim's job to provide legal advice and counsel to SFUSD. The requests for sanctions by both parties are DENIED.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at pvanzandt@aghwlaw.com. The submitted proposed order is to show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem Peter Van Zandt: Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
